name: 𝙋𝙏D 𝙄𝙍S 𝙍eli𝗲f - F𝗼rt W𝗼rth
description_short: Fort Worth tax law firm specializing in IRS tax relief, penalty abatement, and tax dispute resolution for individuals and businesses facing federal and state tax problems.
description_long:
Partners Tax Defense is a Fort Worth-based tax law firm that markets itself as a comprehensive tax resolution service provider. The firm positions itself as offering expert legal assistance for complex tax matters at both federal and state levels. According to their website, they maintain regular business hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-8 PM) and offer free consultations to prospective clients seeking tax guidance.

The firm's stated service offerings include tax audit preparation and representation, civil penalty relief negotiation, federal and state tax dispute resolution, offer in compromise arrangements, tax lien resolution, wage garnishment relief, tax or bank levy resolution, passport reinstatement, innocent spouse issue resolution, and penalty abatement for non-filing cases. They claim expertise in handling both IRS matters and Texas Department of Revenue issues. The website emphasizes their ability to dispute penalties ranging from 5% to 25% monthly for late filing, negotiate installment plans, and provide representation during tax audits involving clerical errors, undeclared income, and deduction disputes.

Partners Tax Defense differentiates itself by highlighting their team composition (attorneys and enrolled agents), promise of customized legal assistance, and claim of extensive experience with both federal and state tax law. Their marketing emphasizes stress reduction and prevention of future tax problems. The firm presents itself as equipped to handle complex tax scenarios including fraud-related penalties and multiple penalty types simultaneously.

A critical assessment reveals this is a tax resolution law firm, not a bankruptcy specialist, though the company was initially categorized as bankruptcy. The website contains no mention of bankruptcy filing services, Chapter 7 proceedings, Chapter 13 plans, or insolvency representation. The firm focuses exclusively on tax resolution, penalty relief, and IRS dispute management. Consumers seeking bankruptcy legal services would be better served by firms explicitly offering bankruptcy filing and discharge procedures.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers free initial consultations for tax problem evaluation
Team includes both tax attorneys and enrolled agents with federal credentials
Handles both federal IRS and Texas state tax matters in one firm
Provides specific penalty relief strategies including reasonable cause arguments and first-time abatement
Advertises expertise in passport reinstatement, a specialized relief service for non-filers
Extended business hours (8 AM-8 PM weekdays) for working professionals
Multiple contact methods: phone (844-548-2374) and email readily available
cons: Website does not clearly distinguish between attorney services and enrolled agent services or explain scope differences
No pricing information, fee structures, or estimated costs disclosed for any service category
No client testimonials, case results, or success metrics provided to verify claims of effectiveness
Miscategorized on CreditDoc as bankruptcy despite offering zero bankruptcy services
Website lacks information about specific credentials, years in practice, or attorney bar standings
best_for: Self-employed individuals and business owners facing IRS audits or undisclosed income issues
Taxpayers with accumulated tax debt seeking offer in compromise or installment arrangements
Non-filers facing substantial penalties and wage garnishment or passport revocation threats
diagnosis: Partners Tax Defense is appropriate for individuals with significant federal or state tax liabilities, audit disputes, or penalty issues who need legal representation before the IRS or Texas tax authorities. The critical caveat: this firm specializes exclusively in tax resolution and dispute management—they do not provide bankruptcy filing services, and consumers seeking insolvency relief should seek bankruptcy counsel elsewhere.
services: Federal and state tax dispute resolution
Offer in compromise negotiation and application
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
